The top 10 on the PGA Tour FedEx Cup points standings, led by No.
Scheffler will be the heavy favorite to stay in first place.
He won The Memorial last year by four shots over Ben Griffin, his second victory in a row at the tournament hosted by Jack Nicklaus.
Unlike last week, when Ludvig Åberg was the only player among the top 10 on the points list to play the Schwab Challenge, 29 of the top 30 on the current standings will tee it up at Muirfield Village.
Russell Henley won the Schwab Challenge but missed getting into the top 10 on the points list by one spot.
He went from 26th to 11th, with 500 points.
As a Signature event, the winner will earn 700 FedEx Cup points.
There is a cut to the low 50, plus ties, so if Scheffler fails to make the weekend, two players can pass him, No.
Scheffler has made 75 cuts in a row, the longest current streak on the PGA Tour, and has finished among the top 25 32 tournaments in a row.
